2605.15763.title: CompactQE: Interpretable Translation Quality Estimation via Small Open-Weight LLMs
2605.15763.paper_summary: Current state-of-the-art Quality Estimation (QE) in machine translation relies on massive, proprietary LLMs, raising data privacy concerns. We demonstrate that smaller, open-source LLMs (<30B parameters) are a viable, cost-effective and privacy-preserving alternative. Using a single-pass prompting strategy, our models simultaneously generate quality scores, MQM error annotations, suggested error corrections, and full post-editions. Our analysis shows these models achieve highly competitive system-level correlations with human judgments that outperform traditional neural metrics, fine-tuned models, and human inter-annotator agreement, effectively approximating the capabilities of much larger proprietary LLMs.
2605.15763.paper_summary_zh: 目前最先進的機器翻譯品質評估（QE）依賴於龐大的專有大型語言模型（LLMs），這引發了數據隱私的擔憂。<lb/>我們證明了較小的開源大型語言模型（<30B 參數）是一種可行的、具成本效益且能保護隱私的替代方案。<lb/>使用單次提示策略，我們的模型同時生成品質分數、MQM 錯誤註釋、建議的錯誤修正以及完整的後編輯。<lb/>我們的分析顯示這些模型在系統級別上與人類判斷的相關性非常競爭，超越了傳統神經度量、微調模型和人類標註者之間的一致性，有效地接近了更大專有大型語言模型的能力。
2605.15763.author: Kamil Guttmann et.al.
2605.15763.authors: Kamil Guttmann, Zofia Fraś, Artur Nowakowski, Krzysztof Jassem
